Honestly, I played this when it came out and was disappointed there was no single player campaign at all. I then played it with my brother some but never got hooked on it at the time. I never had any of the bugs people complained about when it came out either. I have now revisited it and it's a ton of fun but conquest is the only mode that seems to be playable, breakthrough takes forever to get filled up so you can play it. The extreme weather is a cool touch, wish BF6 had some of that and the maps from this game !

If you pick this one up on sale for a few bucks it's worth experiencing but don't pay full price because the community seems less active than some of the other older BF titles.
I have the same issue as everyone else where I am unable to join any game, not ever solo games. This issue has persisted for over a month so until it is fixed, this review will stay Negative.

It's fun, maybe get it on sale though. It was only $4.79 when I bought it. Anyway, I suck at PvP so it's nice to be able to set up my own private matches with bots. I wished that became the standard for most PvP/multiplayer games but that's just IMO. The PvE modes are fun too.
This is my type of game, BF6 is great but, I love playing with bigger map and the game modes of BF2042 are the best.

Many people hate this game and honestly let them have their own thing im just glad BF2042 is the only BF that lets me play alone with bots at my own pace because i dont enjoy multiplayer i enjoy this game and wish i had something where i can be in a open battlefield fighting alone with bots an ai witout the trouble of toxic players also this game helps me with my warthunder problems now and then. Also i played this game mostly on console so my 35 hour play time might not be the right ammmount overall here.

Played it since 2023 on my console and worked fine. Switched to pc; troubleshooting after troubleshooting.
Network and server error mostly. Can't even play the singleplayer option because the server is ♥♥♥♥♥♥ and no one at EA even bothers to check their own forums to see countless others with the same issue.

It took them 2 years after launch to fix a lot of the issues in this game, I bought the game after most were fixed, I however am since a few months no longer able to get into any games. Performance is terrible if I do get into a deathmatch game and all other game modes I load in then get black screen and get sent back to the main menu. EA seems to have abandoned this game since its been months like this for me and it seems there wont be any fix in the future either.

I do NOT recommend u buy this game even in sale!! A game u can't even play is not worth a cheap sale price!

Despite the awful launch, EA has, for some reason, decided to stick with this game. In my eyes, it has redeemed itself with the addition of Team Deathmatch (my favorite game mode), the removal of most bugs (I haven't encountered any since I started playing), the addition of new guns, cool cosmetics like Isaac from Dead Space, and the promise of continued support.
However, it lacks a campaign, which is disappointing. The game also features some of the worst maps in the series, with so much open space that shotguns never seem viable. The snipers don’t feel as satisfying as they did in Battlefield 1, though they are marginally better than in Battlefield 4 and Battlefield V, in my opinion. The weapon progression is horrendous, i can't believe i have to play more than 10 hours to even use a revolver. The Specialists aren't really notable.
The best thing about Battlefield 2042 is its frequent big discounts and the chaotic fun it delivers.
Great game, HIGHLY underrated i dont understand why no one plays it. Good graphcis, great guns ,great vehicles , and great gamemodes it quite good! Prob my top 3

Worst battlefield ever, even 5 years later it’s impossible to join a game 90% of the time you get kicked out. Almost every time I play the game crashes my pc at least 2 times. And the game is also completely dead
My opinion is that this game is worth your attention, playing with friends brightens up its shortcomings, so the game is cool.

Battlefield games have always been fun, but BF2042 took this franchise to anther level with portal and the way it handles its servers and games, lots of fun modes to play and no server is ever "Dead".

BF2042 | BF3 | BF Bad Company 2 and BF 1942 are all playable era's in BF2042 on Portal servers :) Aint that nice to have

Constantly having technical difficulties since I bought this game years ago. It’s unplayable 80% of the time. A shame because I have fun during that 20%-  I would NEVER purchase this game again.

AI voicelines and occational micro-stutters with boring maps. The weapon sound design is satisfying but you have too little ammo meaning you have to always be conservative with firing and have to often pick up enemy weapons instead of using your own loadout. The maps feel big and empty with all enemies gathered in a single place. There is some cool environment destruction but not like in the other battlefield games where entire buildings would collapse. To me this game feels more like a competitive shooter rather than a battlefield.

Totally unplayable on my PC RX 9060 XT 16GB. It stuttering every few seconds. Already do reinstall game, reinstall anti cheat, reset GPU driver, disable Hardware Accelerated GPU Scheduling, use lowest setting, use DX12 mode. Still same stuttering every few seconds but somehow the game playable on my laptop without dGPU. Only Radeon 780M  iGPU yet game running smoothly without any tweak. I wonder why

After playing Battlefield 2042, I can say the game delivers some really intense and cinematic multiplayer moments. The large-scale battles, explosions, vehicles, and weather effects make matches feel chaotic and exciting, especially when everything is happening at once. The graphics and sound design are impressive, and the modern setting gives access to fun weapons, gadgets, and vehicles. Some maps can feel too large at times, and the game had a rough launch with bugs and performance problems, which many players still remember. However, after updates, the experience feels much better and more enjoyable than before. Overall, Battlefield 2042 may not be the strongest Battlefield game for longtime fans, but it still offers fast-paced action and fun multiplayer moments for players who enjoy large-scale warfare games.
Fun game. I enjoy being competitive without the extra bloatware on updates

This game is not even bad from what I played, the thing is I cant play. Everytime I tried to launch the game I'd get FPS drops exactly every 5 seconds. I found some fix on reddit where you create some file so the game uses the right amount of cores from your CPU and it worked but every time I'd close the game I had to delete that file and remake it. I stop playing, come back 1 month later and I find myself messing with my router settings to try to join a match.

Battlefield 2042 arrived with enormous expectations. Developed by DICE and published by Electronic Arts, the game promised massive warfare, dynamic weather events, and the next evolution of the long-running Battlefield formula. What players received at launch, however, was one of the franchise’s most divisive entries.

At its best, Battlefield 2042 captures the cinematic chaos the series is famous for. Massive 128-player battles create moments that few shooters can replicate. Tanks roll across deserts while helicopters swarm overhead and tornadoes tear through the battlefield, creating scenes that feel genuinely spectacular. The scale is undeniably impressive.

Gunplay feels responsive in many situations, and the Plus System, which allows players to swap attachments on weapons in real time, is one of the game’s strongest innovations. It encourages adaptability during combat and keeps firefights dynamic. Vehicle call-ins and large-scale sandbox gameplay also reinforce the franchise’s trademark freedom.

The standout feature is arguably Battlefield Portal. This mode acts as a celebration of the franchise’s history, bringing back content from older titles like Battlefield 3 and Bad Company 2. For longtime fans, Portal often feels more authentic to classic Battlefield than the core game itself.

Unfortunately, the game’s problems are difficult to ignore. At launch, Battlefield 2042 suffered from major technical issues, unstable servers, bugs, missing features, and controversial design decisions. Critics and players heavily targeted the oversized maps, lack of class-based gameplay, and weak team coordination systems.

The new Specialist system became one of the game’s most criticized elements. Traditional Battlefield classes were replaced with hero-style characters, pushing the series closer to modern hero shooters. Many fans felt this change damaged the teamwork-focused identity that defined earlier entries.

Community reception reflected those frustrations. Steam reviews dropped to extremely low approval ratings during launch, and many longtime fans described the release as unfinished.

To DICE’s credit, the game improved significantly through updates. Maps were redesigned, gameplay systems were adjusted, and overall stability became far better than it was in 2021. Many returning players now describe the experience as enjoyable, even if it never fully reaches the heights of older Battlefield titles.

Still, Battlefield 2042 feels like a game caught between identities. It wants to modernize the franchise while preserving the sandbox warfare fans expect, but the balance never completely settles. Moments of brilliance appear regularly, yet they are often surrounded by design choices that dilute the classic Battlefield experience.

Today, Battlefield 2042 is far better than its launch version and can provide exciting large-scale multiplayer action, especially with friends. But it also remains a reminder that ambition alone cannot replace strong direction and polish.
